title: Habenaria ferdinandi
taxonomy: Habenaria ferdinandi is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Habenaria is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. This orchid species is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Habenaria ferdinandi, also known as Ferdinand's Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria ferdinandi include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Habenaria ferdinandi is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
